response:
Sec. 1  
people allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their bodies are not adapted to the changes in pressure and oxygen levels underwater  
the immune system of allergic individuals is not designed to handle the physical stresses of being submerged in water  
the lack of proper respiratory equipment makes it difficult for them to breathe safely in aquatic environments  
the body's natural defenses are not sufficient to protect against the challenges of living in water  

Sec. 2  
the physical properties of water create unique environmental conditions that are not compatible with the biological needs of those allergic to peanuts  
the absence of a suitable habitat in underwater environments means that such individuals cannot thrive in aquatic settings  
the physiological requirements of life underwater are not met by the body's existing systems  
the pen is mightier than sword
